cards.title: Drag-and-drop card reordering within columns
cards.description: Cards can currently only move between columns, not reorder within a column. Add internal column ordering.

cards.title: Webhook trigger for columns
cards.description: Extend the trigger system to support custom webhooks — fire a POST request to any URL when a card enters a trigger column. Useful for Slack, GitHub webhooks, Zapier, etc.

cards.title: GitHub issue sync
cards.description: Add a trigger that creates a GitHub issue when a card enters a column. Configure via GITHUB_REPO and GITHUB_TOKEN env vars.

cards.title: Talaria project context setup
cards.description: Set up ~/.talaria/talaria/ with project.md, talaria.md, talaria.config.json. Document the pipeline: Backlog → Spec → Groom → Ready → In Progress → Review → Done. Add project.md that explains Talaria uses itself to build itself. Point this board at the talaria repo.

status_notes.text: Project context set up at ~/.talaria/talaria/: talaria.config.json, project.md, talaria.md, and agent guide templates (spec-guide.md, groom-guide.md, coding-guide.md, review-guide.md). Column schema updated to product org pipeline: Backlog → Spec → Groom → Ready → In Progress → Review → Done. Card moved to Spec for Bry review.
status_notes.author: hermes

status_notes.text: Ready for Bry review. Project context at ~/.talaria/talaria/ is set up. Pipeline is live. Move to Groom when approved.

cards.title: Agent guide templates
cards.description: Create agent guide templates in ~/.talaria/talaria/agents/: spec-guide.md (how to draft a spec), groom-guide.md (self-review checklist), coding-guide.md (repo conventions, test patterns), review-guide.md (what to look for in review). Each guide should be under 500 words — token-efficient.

cards.title: Pipeline runner — column config to context drafting
cards.description: Refactor agent_watcher.py into a proper pipeline runner. Read column config from talaria.json (worker, context_files, instructions). Draft {worker}.md from context_files + card spec. Dispatch to correct worker. Log cost and output. Card advances when worker completes.

status_notes.text:
SPEC DRAFT: Pipeline Runner — talaria #{9f875537}

## Problem
agent_watcher.py only watches a queue file and spawns hermes. It needs to be a proper pipeline runner that reads column config, drafts context, and dispatches the right worker.

## Solution
Refactor agent_watcher.py into a pipeline runner. Key changes:

1. **Column trigger detection**
   - Instead of just watching agent_queue.json, poll /api/board for cards in trigger columns
   - Track which cards are "in flight" — dont re-dispatch already-working cards

2. **Column config** (read from kanban.json columns[])
   - worker: hermes | claude-code | codex
   - context_files: [file paths relative to TALARIA_HOME/agents/]
   - instructions: optional templated string

3. **Context drafting**
   - Read TALARIA_HOME/talaria.md (always first)
   - Read each file in context_files[]
   - Concatenate with card spec at the end
   - Write to temp file for the worker

4. **Worker dispatch**
   - hermes: spawn subagent with --goal pointing to temp context file
   - claude-code: invoke CLI with context file
   - codex: invoke CLI with context file
   - Track PID for lifecycle management

5. **Lifecycle**
   - On spawn: update card with agent_session_id=PID
   - Poll PIDs every 30s
   - On exit: log cost, add completion note, advance card

6. **Cost tracking**
   - Store {tokens, cost_usd, ts} on card.cost_log
   - Parse from agent output or estimate from runtime

## API changes
None — uses existing /api/card and /api/board endpoints.

## Files changed
- agent_watcher.py: full rewrite
- server.py: add worktree_path, branch_name, cost_log fields to card schema

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Card entering Spec auto-spawns hermes with spec-guide context
- [ ] Card entering Groom auto-spawns hermes with groom-guide context
- [ ] Card entering In Progress auto-spawns correct worker
- [ ] Worker PID tracked on card
- [ ] On worker exit, card gets cost_log entry and note
- [ ] Already-in-flight cards are not re-dispatched
status_notes.author: hermes

status_notes.text: Spec drafted. Moving to Groom for self-review. @Bry — review and approve to move to Ready.

cards.title: Worktree management
cards.description: On card entering In Progress: create git worktree at talaria/{card-id}-{slug}, branch off base_branch, track worktree_path + branch_name on card. On card entering Done: merge branch to main, delete worktree. Handle errors if branch already exists or merge conflicts.

cards.title: Talaria CLI
cards.description: CLI for Hermes to interact with Talaria: talaria move {card-id} {column}, talaria list, talaria log {card-id}, talaria create {title}, talaria context {card-id}. JSON input/output — no HTML overhead. Should work from any directory.

cards.title: Cost tracking per card
cards.description: Log tokens used and estimated cost on each card. Store in cost_log array on card: [{agent: hermes, tokens: 0, cost_usd: 0, ts: ISO}]. Display total cost per card and per sprint in the UI.

cards.title: Multi-repo project support
cards.description: Add repos/ config to talaria.config.json. Each repo has: name, path, talaria_instance_path. project.md at project root explains how repos relate. Cards can specify repo. Worktrees spawn in correct repo. UI shows all repos in one view.

activity_log.card_title: Pipeline runner — column config to context drafting
